pattern: Bucket people by their required group size; greedily fill each bucket and emit a complete group whenever it reaches the target size.
hints: People who share the same required group size must be packed together — collect indices per size in a hash map.
Drain each bucket in fixed-size chunks: as soon as you have accumulated enough indices for one full group, record it and start a new chunk.
The problem guarantees a valid assignment exists, so every bucket's length is divisible by its key; emit groups of exactly that size.

edge_inputs.description: everyone is in a group of size 1
edge_inputs.description2: each person is their own group

edge_inputs.description: everyone is in one big group
mixed sizes requiring multiple separate groupings

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    # build a valid groupSizes by choosing a divisor of n or building from groups
    result = []
    remaining = n
    while remaining > 0:
        # pick a group size that divides cleanly into remaining
        max_s = min(remaining, 20)
        gs = rng.randint(1, max_s)
        # round down to a multiple of gs
        count = (remaining // gs) if remaining >= gs else 1
        actual = gs * count if remaining >= gs else remaining
        if actual == 0:
            actual = 1
            gs = 1
        result.extend([gs] * actual)
        remaining -= actual
    rng.shuffle(result)
    return [result]
